pue_source: Applied Digital Completes Phase II Ready for Service at Polaris Forge 1
notes: PUE 1.18, closed-loop waterless DTC cooling, WUE near zero. CoreWeave anchor tenant. 250MW total planned. Equipment vendor opportunity for cooling infrastructure.
TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.15, water-based liquid cooling, 7MW. Floating barge design with patented natural water cooling. Zero water consumption, no chillers. Supports 55kW+/rack. Ideal Solar Steam target.

pue_source: Inside SUPERNAP 8: Switch's Tier IV Data Fortress
notes: PUE 1.18 with proprietary T-SCIF air cooling. No liquid cooling yet — retrofit opportunity as AI tenants demand higher density.

pue_source: Crusoe announces flagship Abilene data center is live
notes: Massive AI campus with zero-water DTC liquid cooling. Closed-loop system. Phase 1 live, expanding to 1.2GW across 4M sqft. Equipment vendor opportunity at scale.
DeltaFlow liquid cooling supporting DTC, RDHx, and immersion up to 300kW/rack. Seamless air-to-liquid transition in live environments. 50+ campuses, 5GW+ planned.

pue_source: EdgeConneX and Lambda To Build AI Factory In Chicago With Industry-Leading High-Density Data Center Infrastructure
notes: 23MW AI factory with hybrid liquid-to-chip + air cooling for Lambda. Part of 30MW+ dual-city deal (Chicago + Atlanta). Greenfield.

pue_source: GRC Expands Immersion Cooling to the Edge with New ICEraQ Nano System
notes: GRC immersion cooling partnership with Endor Development for giga-scale AI DCs. ICEraQ technology. PUE 1.02-1.05 typical for immersion. First deployments H2 2026. Competitive intelligence.
TRIPLE MATCH: PUE 1.1, wall-to-wall liquid, 9MW. 250kW/cabinet.
0.5MW small colo. NVIDIA DGX-Ready certified. Liquid cooling ready.
36MW with built-in liquid cooling. PUE 1.3. High-density compute focus.
54MW, 100kW+/rack, liquid-cooled or hybrid. PUE 1.35. Coming online 2026.
PUE 1.04 — lowest verified in dataset. TIER 0 facilities. 41% more efficient than Tier 3. Wind/solar/carbon-free energy mix. Originally crypto, pivoting to AI.
PUE 1.15. ThermalWorks waterless cooling. 200kW/rack liquid plug-and-play. 13.2MW. Modular design.
PUE 1.15. 26MW. ThermalWorks waterless. 200kW/rack liquid. Nuclear power partnership announced.
7.5MW with liquid cooling. ≤10MW target. Atlanta market. Air + liquid systems.

GRC ICEraQ immersion cooling Frontera GPU subsystem. 11+ year relationship. COMPETITOR INTEL — GRC's flagship HPC reference.
WORLD'S LOWEST VERIFIED PUE: 1.036. Warm water DLC captures 97% waste heat. LEED Platinum. Chiller-less design.
World's fastest supercomputer. PUE 1.08. 100% fanless DLC. 40K AMD MI300A. Saves $12M/yr electricity.
PUE 1.06-1.07. Google's most efficient US site. ~50% of Google fleet has liquid cooling. 1GW liquid across 2000 TPU pods.
205MW with proprietary in-house DLC. 180kW/rack. Competitive intel — built own cooling.
Government/military GRC immersion. ICEraQ + ElectroSafe fluid. Modular. COMPETITOR INTEL.
10MW dedicated inference. 300+ Cerebras CS-3 Wafer-Scale Engines. Air-cooled (non-GPU arch). Unique compute.
9MW (qualifies!). DLC at >80kW/rack. CoreWeave tenant. Oregon hydro power.
WORLD'S LARGEST single-site AI. 2GW. 555K GPUs. Custom DLC for 200K GPUs. Built in 122 days.
World's most powerful AI DC. 4,608 GB300 NVL72. 92.1 exaFLOPS FP4. Custom HXU DLC. Zero water.
100MW (→300MW). GB200 NVL72. DLC 130kW/rack. First GB300 commercially deployed.
10MW DLC integration facility. Builds liquid-cooled racks for enterprise.
60MW (6x10MW halls). 10MW halls qualify! Near-zero water DLC. 12K gallons total.

10MW (qualifies!). Air+liquid. GPU-capable. T5 delivered 40K sqft DLC in 12 months.
90% of tenants liquid-cooled! 1,500+ B200 GPUs. 100kW+/cabinet. CoreSite = American Tower.
1.4GW! 700K+ liquid-cooled GPUs capacity. $9.7B Microsoft contract. Energizing April 2026!
$25B. 1.4GW. 10 buildings. Liquid cooling for GPUs. Closed-loop.
$5B JV. Proprietary DLC (2x air efficiency). CoreWeave anchor. 350 acres Virginia.

$500M. 10K+ Blackwell Ultra. Scaling to 100MW+. Microsoft deal.

DLC-native campus. Cold plate+sidecars+immersion. PUE ~1.2. Hydropower. Lowest TCO.

facility_name: Nebius Kansas City AZ (Patmos AI Campus)
notes: Initial 5MW phase operational since Q1 2025 with H200/B200/B300 GPUs, liquid-ready hybrid cooling with 3 cooling towers and district distilled water. Expanding to 40MW by late 2026. Small initial deployment is prime target for cooling vendor engagement before scale-up.

3MW facility hosting Groq LPU Inference Engines. LPU is currently air-cooled but TierPoint offers liquid cooling capability for future high-density requirements. Next-gen Groq LPX rack will require liquid cooling. Small facility, strong candidate for cooling upgrade path.

facility_name: Oregon State University CEOAS Data Center (ZutaCore Retrofit)
notes: University research data center retrofitted with ZutaCore HyperCool waterless two-phase direct-to-chip cooling. 30% lower processor temps, 20% higher compute performance. Eliminated 4 air cooling systems. Heat recaptured for campus building heating. Heterogeneous fleet (Dell, HP, Supermicro with NVIDIA/AMD). University HPC centers are typically under 5MW, making this a strong small-facility indicator.

facility_name: Dataprana AI Data Center — Galveston County
notes: Ultra-small 1.5MW AI data center in Galveston County, TX. Joint venture with DC Futureminds. Dataprana specializes in immersion-cooled facilities (flagship 30MW immersion site in La Marque, TX). This is a proof-of-concept site targeting GPUaaS providers. Extremely small facility — ideal for small-scale cooling vendor engagement.

facility_name: UT Arlington ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS Data Center Testbed
notes: DOE-funded data center cooling testbed at UT Arlington, led by Prof. Dereje Agonafer (National Academy of Engineering). Accelsius MR250 250kW two-phase CDU deployed. Target: reduce cooling energy from 40% to under 5% of total IT load. Proof of concept by H1 2026. Partners include JetCool, Intel, Raytheon, HP, NVIDIA, Flexnode. University-scale facility likely under 5MW.

PUE 1.1 + liquid cooling at 20MW. Colovore's third facility, purpose-built for high-density AI/HPC with up to 250kW per rack via DTC and rear-door cooling. Groundbreaking Feb 2025. Larger than 10MW threshold but PUE and cooling match strongly. Colovore is a liquid-cooling-native operator — competitor landscape intelligence value.

PUE ~1.1 + liquid cooling at 20MW. Fourth Colovore location, pre-leasing now for 2027 launch. Larger than 10MW but matches on PUE and cooling. Pre-construction phase means cooling vendor decisions still open.

facility_name: Sabey SDC Austin — TACC Horizon Supercomputer
notes: Sabey's first Texas campus at 85MW total, with 15-20MW allocated to TACC Horizon (largest academic supercomputer in NSF portfolio). Dell-built system using 100% direct liquid cooling with 140kW/cabinet GPU density and immersion cooling. 6x more energy-efficient than air-cooled. 300 petaflops peak FP64. Spring 2026 launch. Campus total is large but TACC deployment is in 15-20MW range.

facility_name: Equinix DC15 CIF — Accelsius NeuCool Deployment
notes: Equinix's Co-Innovation Facility at DC15 Ashburn with Accelsius NeuCool IR80 two-phase DTC system deployed Q3 2025. Partnership catalyzed by shared ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS participation. NeuCool allows 6-8C warmer inlet water = more free cooling. CIF is a showcase/testbed but signals Equinix's cooling vendor evaluation pipeline. Competitive intelligence value for cooling vendors.

Colovore's 'Project Raptor' — $500M investment on 30-acre Ironwood Tract near Austin. Part of Colovore's expansion from 10MW to 50MW total. PUE 1.1 (company standard). Liquid cooling + closed-loop water. Construction early 2026. Pre-construction = ideal timing for cooling vendor engagement.

Ultra-small 1MW edge data center in Long Island City, NYC. ARPA-E COOLERCHIPS-funded ($3.5M) technology: manifold microchannel heatsink + hybrid immersion cooling + additive-manufactured dry cooler. 80kW capable racks with precision immersion and cold plate. GPU and CPU solutions. Digital twin running. Company plans 'a couple more' US sites. Ideal small-facility target.

PUE 1.2 + liquid cooling at 15.2MW. Metrobloks is a 2024-founded developer specializing in high-density liquid-cooled modular colocation in metro areas. Miami facility features up to 150kW/rack, hybrid air + DTC liquid cooling, 22MW reserved utility power. Q4 2026 launch. Ultra-low latency to NAP of the Americas. Pre-construction = cooling vendor decisions open.
